DISCOVERY 610, PLAINTIFFS TATIANA ALEXANDROVA AND ALEXANDRIA MCDOWELL'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND FURTHER RESPONSE TO DOCUMENT REQUESTS, FURTHER RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES, FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST FOR ADMISSION; REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTION; BASED ON THE PARTIES MEET AND CONFER AGREEMENTS, DEFENDANT SHALL SERVE VERIFIED, SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES AND PRODUCE DOCUMENTS, AS AGREED, ON OR BEFORE DECEMBER 21, 2009. HEARING CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 28, 2009 AT 9:00 A.M. IF DEFENDANT IS IN COMPLIANCE, THE PARTIES NEED NOT APPEAR; AND THE MOTION WILL GO OFF CALENDAR. IF ISSUES REMAIN, THE PARTIES SHALL INFORM THE COURT, BY DECEMBER 23, 2009, PROVIDING A LETTER OR MEMORANDUM THAT SUMMARIZES THE OUTSTANDING ISSUES FOR THE COURT'S CONSIDERATION. COMMISSIONER: PAUL SLAVIT. NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Re Depositions And Request For Sanc Tions In The Ampount Of $3675.00; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Apr-27-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Matthew Stephenson, Atty. for Plaintiff and moving party: Tatiana Alexandrova
Shirley Wang, Atty. for defendant and moving party: Stink, Inc.

RULING: HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT STINK, INC., JUAN BECERRA's Motion For Protective Order re: Depositions is moot as the court made an order on April 8, 2010 requiring future depositions to take place at the courthouse. The motion is denied as it seeks an order that Plaintiff's deposition take place without an interpreter. All requests for sanctions are denied. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.

Shirley C. Wang, Esq., and Marguerite Meade, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Stink, Inc.

Notice Of Motion For Protective Order Re; Defense Counsel'S Deposition And Request For A Monetary Sanction As Against Deft. Stink, Inc. And Marguerite Meade Of The Davis Law Firm In The Amount Of 5,080.00
Off Calendar Apr-08-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Mathew Stephenson, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Tatiana Alexandrova
Shirley C. Wang, Esq., and Marguerite Meade, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Stink, Inc.

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiffs' Motion For Protective Order Re: Defense Counsel's Deposition Conduct is granted as future depositions shall take place in the courthouse. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses And Production Of Documents In Response To Deft'S Request For Production Of Documents (Set One) And Request For Monetary Sanctions Aginst Pltf'S Counsel In The Amount Of $2140.00
Off Calendar Jan-28-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Marguerite E. Meade, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Stink, Inc., and Juan Becerra
Deborah Kochan, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Tatiana Lexandrova and Alexandra McDowell
After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted as modified.
Defendant Stink, Inc. and Juan Becerra's Motion To Compel Further Responses And Production Of Documents In Response To Defendant's Request For Production Of Documents (Set One) is denied as to No. 28 as it is irrelevant and overly broad.
As to the remainder of the responses, the objections based upon privacy and physician-patient privilege are overruled and ordered stricken as set forth in the rulings to the form interrogatories. As to Nos. 2, 3, and 6, the responses are properly limited to documents related to the allegations of Plaintiff's complaint.
Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order.
Defendant's request for sanctions is denied.
MODIFICATION: Nos. 29 and 30 limited to the subject matter of Plaintiff's complaint, financial documents claimed to be privileged shall be identified in a privilege log.
Commissioner Everett A. Hewllett. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories-Employment (Set One) And Request For Attorneys Fees Against Pltf'S In The Amount Of $1090.00
Off Calendar Jan-28-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Marguerite E. Meade, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Stink, Inc., and Juan Becerra
Deborah Kochan, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Tatiana Lexandrova and Alexandra McDowell
After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendants' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories'Employment (Set One) is granted as to Nos. 212.4, 212..5, 212.6, and 212.7. If the responses are no, there is no invasion of privacy. If the responses are yes, they are relevant and any right to privacy is waived by their relevance to this action.
Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order.
Defendant's request for sanctions is denied. Commissioner Everett A. Hewllett. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories - General (Set No. One) And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf'S Counsel In The Amount Of $1090.00
Off Calendar Jan-28-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Marguerite E. Meade, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Stink, Inc., and Juan Becerra
Deborah Kochan, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Tatiana Lexandrova and Alexandra McDowell
After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant Stink, Inc. and Juan Becerra's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories ' General (Set One) is granted as to Nos. 6.4. 6.5, 6.6, 6.7, 10.1, and 10.3. Plaintiff's objections are overruled and ordered stricken.
The motion is granted as to No. 10.2, limited to the period six months prior to the first incident that is a part of the allegations of the complaint. Plaintiff's objections are overruled and ordered stricken.
The motion is granted as to Nos. 12.1 and 12.3. Plaintiff's objections are overruled and ordered stricken.
Plaintiff is ordered to respond within 15 days of notice of this order.
Defendant's request for sanctions is denied. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Marguerite E. Meade, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Stink, Inc.

Hearing held on Plaintiff Tatiana Alexandrova's Motion to compel production of documents and further response to document requests, further responses to interrogatories, further response to request for admission; request for monetary sanction.
The parties reach agreement with the court's assistance. Plaintiff will draft and submit a proposed order. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Further Responses To Document Requests, Further Responses To Interrogatories, Further Respones To Requests For Admission, And Request For Monetary Sanction
Off Calendar Dec-14-2009 Continued To Dec-28-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiffs Tatiana Alexandrova and Alexandria McDowell's Motion to compel production of documents and further response to document requests, further responses to interrogatories, further response to request for admission; request for monetary sanction; based on the parties meet and confer agreements, defendant shall serve verified, supplemental responses and produce documents, as agreed, on or before December 21, 2009. Hearing continued to December 28, 2009 at 9:00 a.m. If defendant is in compliance, the parties need not appear; and the motion will go off calendar. If issues remain, the parties shall inform the court, by December 23, 2009, providing a letter or memorandum that summarizes the outstanding issues for the court's consideration. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Pmk Deposition And Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-25-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Mathew Stephenson, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Tatiana Alexandrova
Marguerite Meade, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Stink, Inc.

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted as modified.
Plaintiffs Tatiana Alexandrova and Alexandria McDowell's Motion to compel PMK deposition and production of documents and request for monetary sanctions granted in part, denied in part:
1. Defendant shall designate and produce for deposition its person(s) most knowledgeable, and documents as specified in plaintiffs' notice of deposition within fifteen days of notice of entry of this order;
2. The deposition shall be held at a place and time to be agreed by the parties, and in accordance with the availability of counsel and the deponent(s);
3. Request for sanctions denied.
4. With reference to document production, responding party shall serve a privilege log within 15 days of notice of entry of this order. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

payments.description: REQ OR STIP AND ORDER W/O HEARING
